notes_for_human_reviewer: BOILERPLATE FAILURE MODE. Items are textbook PM-101 generics that could apply to any project — none reference the specific scenario (vendor v2 deprecation in 90 days, lead engineer on paternity leave 4 weeks, scope creep adding 3 endpoints, premature expansion to 2 more clinics). The grader should recognise the absence of scenario-specific items and floor completeness/differentiation. Mitigation 'standard PM practices' is the canonical platitude. Single owner attribution ('Project Manager') is slightly better than 'project team' but still not specific. Tests whether Claude rewards structure-without-substance — common drift is scoring 2-3 because the format looks correct.
